factors.summary: The company faces risks related to input costs, including price volatility for commodities such as steel, copper, and aluminum, which can lead to cost increases and supply interruptions. While the company has implemented strategies like futures contracts and cost reduction initiatives to mitigate these risks, rising commodity prices and tariffs have contributed to higher input costs, particularly in the Refrigeration segment.

evidence.text: We depend on raw materials, such as steel, copper and aluminum, and components purchased from third parties to manufacture our products. Some of these third-party suppliers are located outside of the United States. If a supplier is unable or unwilling to meet our supply requirements, including suffering any disruptions at its facilities or in its supply chain, we could experience supply interruptions or cost increases, either of which could have an adverse effect on our results of operations.
evidence.subsection: Price Volatility for Commodities and Components We Purchase or Significant Supply Interruptions Could Have an Adverse Effect on Our Cash Flow or Results of Operations.

evidence.text: Higher commodities contributed to cost increases in Refrigeration segment.
evidence.subsection: Year Ended December 31, 2019 Compared to Year Ended December 31, 2018 - Results by Segment

evidence.text: The principal raw materials used in our manufacturing processes are steel, copper and aluminum. In recent years, pricing volatility for these commodities and related components has impacted us and the HVACR industry in general. We seek to mitigate the impact of commodity price volatility through a combination of pricing actions, vendor contracts, improved production efficiency and cost reduction initiatives. We also partially mitigate volatility in the prices of these commodities by entering into futures contracts and fixed forward contracts.
evidence.subsection: Business Overview

sentiment.label: negative
sentiment.rationale: Increased input costs due to rising commodity prices and tariffs.
